# RunStrict

> Last updated: 2026-06-17. Season 5 is live (40-day seasons; the current season number and its dates are shown live at https://runstrict.com). 1,200+ active runners across 8 cities.

> RunStrict is a free GPS running game for iOS, Android, Apple Watch, and Wear OS where runners physically capture hexagonal territory tiles by running through them. Players choose one of three teams — FLAME, WAVE, or CHAOS — and compete to control their city's map before a 40-day season resets everything in an event called The Void. The site is available in 11 languages.

## What is RunStrict?

RunStrict is a location-based running app that gamifies outdoor running. Using GPS tracking and Uber's H3 hexagonal grid system (resolution 9, ~175m per hex), every step a runner takes flips territory on a real city map to their team's color. The last runner through a hex owns it. It is sometimes described as "Strava meets Risk" or a "Pokémon GO for runners," but it requires real running with pace verification.

## Key Features

- **GPS Territory Capture**: Run through ~175m hexagonal tiles to flip them to your team's color in real-time
- **Three Teams**: FLAME (Red), WAVE (Blue), CHAOS (Purple) — each with a distinct buff philosophy (see Teams)
- **Buff Multipliers**: An honest ×1 floor (a no-bonus run scores exactly ×1); maximums are FLAME ×5, WAVE ×4, CHAOS ×4
- **40-Day Seasons**: Every season ends with "The Void" — a complete reset of all hexes, points, and territory. Only the leaderboard legend survives
- **Anti-Cheat**: 25 km/h speed cap, accelerometer verification, GPS accuracy ≤50m required, under 8 min/km pace to capture
- **Battery Optimized**: 0.5 Hz GPS polling, no WebSocket, syncs only on launch/resume/run completion
- **Tiered Leaderboards**: Province, District, and Zone scope rankings
- **Privacy First**: No runner IDs stored in hexes, only team color and timestamp
- **Pace Consistency Score**: CV-based stability metric rewards steady runners
- **Wearables**: Apple Watch and Wear OS companion apps with wrist-first run controls, live pace, and hex-capture alerts

## Platform Availability

- **iOS**: Available now on the App Store (iOS 15+) — https://apps.apple.com/us/app/runstrict/id6760189457
- **Android**: Available now on Google Play (Android 10+) — https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.neondialactic.runstrict
- **Apple Watch**: Available now (watchOS 10+) — companion bundled with the iPhone app
- **Wear OS**: Available now (Wear OS 3+ / SDK 30; Galaxy Watch 4+, Pixel Watch) — companion via Google Play. Tile, complication, and wrist-first run controls; runs survive a dropped phone connection
- **Languages**: The website is localized in 11 languages — English, Korean, Japanese, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese (Brazil), Russian, Arabic, and Hindi

## How It Works

1. Open RunStrict and start running — GPS tracks your route in real-time
2. Maintain under 8 min/km pace to activate hex capture mode
3. Every ~175m hex tile you run through flips to your team's color
4. The last runner through a hex controls it — speed and consistency matter
5. Earn buff multipliers based on your team's mechanics
6. Compete on tiered leaderboards (Zone, District, Province)
7. When the 40-day season ends, The Void wipes everything and a new season begins automatically

## Teams

Buffs are an additive sum of whole-number terms with an honest ×1 floor (the displayed buff equals the scored buff). Friendly refresh (running through a hex your team already owns) earns a flat +1 point for everyone.

### FLAME (Red) — competition
Performance-based meritocracy. The top 20% of each province's Red runners become Elites (+2). Territory base is ×1–3 (district win +1, province win +1). Maximum **×5** for an Elite who also holds both territory wins; common runners earn ×1–3.

### WAVE (Blue) — sharing
Collective strength, everyone equal. Territory base ×1–3 plus a turnout bonus: if ≥50% of your province's Blue ran yesterday, every Blue gets +1. Maximum **×4**, shared equally.

### CHAOS (Purple) — wide sharing
Join anytime, but membership is irreversible. A pure province-participation ladder with no territory terms: ×1 base, ×2 at 25% active, ×3 at 50%, ×4 at 75%. Maximum **×4**.

## Technology

- **Hex Grid**: Uber's H3 geospatial indexing at resolution 9 (~175m hexes); provinces at resolution 4, districts at resolution 6
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + RLS + Edge Functions); serverless, no custom API server
- **Client**: Flutter (iOS, Android, Wear OS) and a native Apple Watch companion
- **Timezone**: Game logic uses GMT+2; seasons roll over at midnight GMT+2

## Company

- **Website**: https://runstrict.com
- **App Store**: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/runstrict/id6760189457
- **Google Play**: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.neondialactic.runstrict
- **Price**: Free
- **Category**: Sports / Running / Location-based Game

## Comparisons

RunStrict differs from traditional running apps (Strava, Nike Run Club, Runkeeper) by adding a competitive territory-capture game layer. Unlike fitness trackers that focus on personal metrics, RunStrict makes every run a strategic move in a team game. It also differs from location games like Pokémon GO by requiring actual physical running (with pace verification) rather than walking.

## FAQ

**Is RunStrict free?**
Yes, completely free to download and play, on both iOS and Android.

**What devices does it support?**
iPhone (iOS 15+), Android (10+), Apple Watch (watchOS 10+), and Wear OS (3+ / SDK 30 — Galaxy Watch 4+, Pixel Watch). All companion apps are available now.

**What are the buff multipliers?**
There is an honest ×1 floor. Maximums are FLAME ×5 (Elite, top 20% of a province), WAVE ×4 (district + province wins + ≥50% team turnout), and CHAOS ×4 (75% province participation).

**Can I cheat with GPS spoofing?**
No. RunStrict uses accelerometer verification, a 25 km/h speed cap, and GPS accuracy requirements to prevent spoofing.

**What happens when a season ends?**
The Void resets all hexes, points, and territory. A new 40-day season begins automatically. Only the leaderboard legend survives.

**Can I switch teams?**
FLAME and WAVE players can switch teams between seasons. CHAOS membership is permanent and irreversible.

**Does it drain battery?**
No. RunStrict uses 0.5 Hz GPS polling (once every 2 seconds) and syncs data only on app launch, resume, and run completion — no persistent connections.

**How big are the hexes?**
Each hex is approximately 175 meters edge-to-edge, using Uber's H3 resolution 9 grid system.

**Where is RunStrict available?**
Globally — territory capture works in any city with GPS coverage. The app is on the App Store and Google Play worldwide, and the website is localized in 11 languages.
